title: helleborine palustris f. ericetorum
intro: Helleborine palustris f. ericetorum, also known as the marsh helleborine, is a rare, subalpine to alpine orchid species. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and being used as a natural remedy for fever reduction. Helleborine palustris f. ericetorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the need for proper identification.
